Package org.eventb.internal.pp

Class PPProverCall

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.seqprover.xprover.AbstractXProverCall
org.eventb.core.seqprover.xprover.XProverCall2
org.eventb.internal.pp.PPProverCall
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable, Cancellable, IPPMonitor
public class PPProverCall extends XProverCall2 implements IPPMonitor
Implementation of XProverCall2 for PP.

  • Constructor Details

    • PPProverCall

      public PPProverCall(PPInput input, ISimpleSequent sequent, IProofMonitor pm)

  • Method Details

    • cleanup

      public void cleanup()
      Description copied from class: AbstractXProverCall
      Cleans up this prover call. This is the place to put any code that does some clean up. For instance, a client could put here the code to delete any intermediate file that was created by this prover call.

      This method will always be called after the task running run() has been canceled. However, as task synchronization is only done cooperatively in Java, there is no guarantee that method run() has actually finished execution when this method is called.

      Specified by:
      cleanup in class AbstractXProverCall

    • displayMessage

      public String displayMessage()
      Description copied from class: AbstractXProverCall
      Returns the human-readable message that will be displayed for this proof.

      This method is called only when AbstractXProverCall.isValid() returned true.

      Specified by:
      displayMessage in class AbstractXProverCall
      Returns:
      the message to display for this proof

    • isValid

      public boolean isValid()
      Description copied from class: AbstractXProverCall
      Tells whether the sequent passed to the constructor has been proved valid by the external prover.

      This method and AbstractXProverCall.run() may be called concurrently. Implementors should not make any assumption on the respective call orders of these two methods.

      Specified by:
      isValid in class AbstractXProverCall
      Returns:
      true if the sequent to prove is valid

    • run

      public void run()
      Description copied from class: AbstractXProverCall
      Run the external prover on the sequent given at instance creation.

      Implementors of this method must take great care to check very often for cancellation using AbstractXProverCall.isCancelled() and to respond to thread interruption in a timely manner. If either cancellation or interruption has been requested, this method shall return as soon as possible.

      Specified by:
      run in interface Runnable
      Specified by:
      run in class AbstractXProverCall

    • isGoalNeeded

      public boolean isGoalNeeded()
      Description copied from class: AbstractXProverCall
      Tells whether the goal was relevant to the proof of the sequent. For instance, if a sequent was discharged because its hypotheses were contradictory, this method should return false.

      However, most provers do not tell whether the goal was relevant to their proof. In that case, one should not override this method as the default will apply.

      This method is called only when AbstractXProverCall.isValid() returned true.

      Overrides:
      isGoalNeeded in class AbstractXProverCall
      Returns:
      false iff the sequent was discharged independently of its goal

    • neededHypotheses

      public Set<Predicate> neededHypotheses()
      Description copied from class: AbstractXProverCall
      Returns the hypotheses that were used to discharge this sequent, or null if they are unknown (the sequent prover will then assume that all the hypotheses of the sequent were needed).

      Most provers do not tell which hypotheses were relevant to their proof. In that case, one should not override this method as the default will apply.

      This method is called only when AbstractXProverCall.isValid() returned true.

      Overrides:
      neededHypotheses in class AbstractXProverCall
      Returns:
      a set containing all the hypotheses that are needed to discharge the sequent to prove, or null if unknown

    • isCanceled

      public boolean isCanceled()
      Description copied from interface: IPPMonitor
      Tells whether this proof has been canceled. This method is regularly called by the Predicate Prover. It it returns true, then the current proof is aborted.
      Specified by:
      isCanceled in interface IPPMonitor
      Returns:
      true iff this proof has been canceled.
